name: Prague
localTips: Validate your public transport ticket in the little yellow machines the moment you board — plainclothes inspectors fine tourists constantly and won't accept 'I just bought it'.
Avoid restaurants with laminated multilingual menus and staff calling out to tourists on Old Town Square — walk two streets back for half the price and real Czech food.
Always check the bill line by line; a handful of tourist-trap restaurants still add unordered 'bread' or 'service' charges — it's illegal but common enough to watch for.
Tap water in Prague is genuinely excellent and safe to drink, so skip paying for bottled water at restaurants.
Beer is often cheaper than water on a menu here — a half-litre of Pilsner Urquell on tap typically costs less than a bottle of Coke.

activities.title: Charles Bridge at dawn
activities.description: Cross the 14th-century Charles Bridge before 7am to have its 30 baroque statues and Vltava views almost to yourself, before the tour groups and portrait painters arrive.

activities.title: Old Town Square & Astronomical Clock
activities.description: Watch the 1410 Astronomical Clock's hourly show of apostles and a skeleton striking the bell, ringed by Týn Church's Gothic spires and pastel baroque façades.

activities.title: Petřín Hill park walk
activities.description: Climb wooded paths past a mini Eiffel Tower lookalike and a mirror maze to the summit for the best skyline view in the city, especially at sunset.

activities.title: Vyšehrad fortress & cemetery
activities.description: A quieter hilltop fortress south of the centre with river-bend views, Romanesque rotunda ruins and the cemetery where Dvořák and Mucha are buried.

activities.title: Letná Park beer garden view
activities.description: A leafy plateau above the river with the city's best free panorama of Charles Bridge and the castle, plus a low-key beer garden where locals watch the sunset.

activities.title: Josefov old Jewish quarter streets
activities.description: Wander the streets of Josefov past the exteriors of the Old-New Synagogue and the leaning gravestones visible over the Old Jewish Cemetery wall, free without a ticket.

activities.title: John Lennon Wall
activities.description: A constantly repainted wall of graffiti and lyrics that became a symbol of youth defiance under communism — bring a marker and add your own line if you like.

activities.title: Havelské Tržiště market
activities.description: A centuries-old open-air market street just off Old Town Square selling fruit, marionettes and trdelník — browse it free, buy only what tempts you.

activities.title: Prague Castle courtyards & St. Vitus exterior
activities.description: The castle's outer courtyards, the changing of the guard, and St. Vitus Cathedral's soaring Gothic facade are all free to see — only the interiors require a ticket.

activities.title: National Gallery Veletržní Palace (free permanent hours)
activities.description: A vast functionalist palace of modern and contemporary Czech art, including Alphonse Mucha's monumental Slav Epic — free entry on the first Wednesday evening of the month.

activities.title: Dancing House (Fred & Ginger building)
activities.description: Frank Gehry's twisting glass-and-concrete building looks like two dancers mid-spin — a striking piece of 1990s architecture right on the Vltava embankment.

activities.title: Old Town Square Christmas market
activities.description: A towering Christmas tree, mulled wine stalls and a nativity scene fill the square from late November — one of Europe's most photographed markets.

activities.title: Riegrovy Sady beer garden sunset
activities.description: A local park on a ridge above Vinohrady with a big self-service beer garden and unbeatable sunset views over the castle skyline.

activities.title: Nusle Bridge & Folimanka park walk
activities.description: A quieter residential walk beneath the huge Nuselský Bridge, through Folimanka park and up toward Vyšehrad — a green route locals use to avoid the tourist crowds entirely.

activities.title: Kampa Island riverside park
activities.description: A small island park below Charles Bridge with the mysterious oversized baby sculptures by David Černý and a quiet stretch of Vltava riverbank.

activities.title: Coffee among locals in Vinohrady
activities.description: The residential Vinohrady district has Prague's best concentration of independent coffee roasters and quiet café terraces, well away from Old Town's tourist prices.
activities.insiderNote: Try Café Sladkovský on Náměstí Jiřího z Poděbrad — same square as the striking Church of the Most Sacred Heart of Our Lord.

activities.title: Cross Club industrial bar
activities.description: A maze of hand-welded steel corridors and gears in Holešovice, part bar and part sculpture, hosting alternative DJs most nights of the week.
activities.insiderNote: Come early evening to explore the metal-pipe architecture properly before the crowd and volume pick up after 10pm.

activities.title: Unpasteurised Pilsner at Lokál
activities.description: A modern take on the classic Czech pub, with tiled walls and long communal tables, pouring tank Pilsner Urquell the way locals insist it should taste.
activities.insiderNote: Order 'tankové pivo' specifically — it's unpasteurised beer delivered fresh from the brewery, noticeably different from the bottled version most tourists get.

activities.title: Náplavka riverside embankment
activities.description: The Rašínovo nábřeží embankment turns into an open-air bar strip most evenings, with converted boats serving drinks and a Saturday farmers' market by day.
activities.insiderNote: Saturday mornings from 8am the farmers' market takes over — get there early for fresh trdelník and Czech cheeses before the crowds.

activities.title: Craft beer at Vzorkovna
activities.description: A rotating-tap craft beer bar in a converted factory sample-room in Karlín, popular with young locals for its ever-changing lineup of small Czech breweries.
activities.insiderNote: Ask the bartender for a taster flight — Czech craft brewing has exploded beyond Pilsner-style lagers and staff love talking through the board.

activities.title: Live jazz at Jazz Dock
activities.description: A glass-walled jazz club built out over the Vltava in Smíchov, with nightly live sets and a floating terrace view of the river — a favourite with Prague's own musicians.
activities.insiderNote: Arrive by 8pm for a window table right on the water; later sets get standing-room only.

activities.title: Farmers' market at Náměstí Jiřího z Poděbrad
activities.description: A twice-weekly farmers' market in a residential Vinohrady square, with Moravian wine, fresh strudel and produce stalls locals actually shop at.
activities.insiderNote: Wednesday and Saturday mornings only — go early for the fresh koláče pastries before the bakery stall sells out.

activities.title: Peek into Strahov Monastery courtyard
activities.description: Beyond the famous baroque libraries (which need a ticket), the monastery's outer courtyard and small on-site brewery pub are free and far less crowded than the castle nearby.
activities.insiderNote: The monastery's own Klášterní pivovar brews its own beer on-site — order the amber St. Norbert lager in the courtyard garden.

activities.title: Astronomical Clock tower climb
activities.description: Climb (or take the lift up) the Old Town Hall tower for the highest viewpoint over the square, right above the crowd watching the clock's hourly show.
activities.bookingQuery: Old Town Hall tower Astronomical Clock ticket Prague

activities.title: Prague Castle full circuit ticket
activities.description: Full access to St. Vitus Cathedral's interior, the Old Royal Palace, St. George's Basilica and Golden Lane's tiny historic houses inside the world's largest ancient castle complex.
activities.bookingQuery: Prague Castle full circuit entry ticket

activities.title: Jewish Museum Prague & synagogues ticket
activities.description: One ticket covers the Old-New Synagogue, the Pinkas Synagogue's wall of Holocaust victims' names and the atmospheric Old Jewish Cemetery's centuries of leaning headstones.
activities.bookingQuery: Jewish Museum Prague synagogues combined ticket

activities.title: Vltava evening dinner cruise
activities.description: Sail past Charles Bridge and the illuminated castle on a glass-topped boat while a three-course dinner is served — Prague's skyline is made for this at night.
activities.bookingQuery: Vltava river evening dinner cruise Prague

activities.title: Czech Beer Museum tasting tour
activities.description: A guided tasting through Czech brewing history and styles, from Pilsner's 1842 invention to today's craft scene, in cellars beneath Old Town.
activities.bookingQuery: Czech Beer Museum tasting tour Prague

activities.title: Kutná Hora & Bone Church day trip
activities.description: An hour outside the city, the Sedlec Ossuary is decorated with the bones of 40,000 people, and the UNESCO old town has one of Bohemia's grandest cathedrals.
activities.bookingQuery: Kutna Hora Bone Church day trip from Prague

activities.title: Black light theatre show
activities.description: A uniquely Czech art form born in 1960s Prague — dancers in UV-lit costumes appear to float against total darkness in a wordless, visually dazzling performance.
activities.bookingQuery: black light theatre show ticket Prague

activities.title: Old Town food & tasting tour
activities.description: Sample svíčková, trdelník, Czech cheeses and local wines across several stops in the historic centre with a guide explaining what makes each dish traditional.
activities.bookingQuery: Old Town food tasting walking tour Prague

activities.title: Strahov Monastery Library entry
activities.description: Two breathtaking baroque libraries — the Theological and Philosophical Halls — packed with frescoes, globes and 200,000 antique volumes behind glass.
activities.bookingQuery: Strahov Monastery Library entry ticket Prague

activities.title: Mucha Museum entry
activities.description: A compact museum dedicated to Alphonse Mucha's swirling Art Nouveau posters and designs, a short walk from Wenceslas Square.
